name: Tuna Salad Sandwich
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 oz (1) can tuna; packed in oil
1 tbsp Green pepper (red or yellow for color if you can)
1 tsp Vinegar (Marukan Seasoned Rice Vinegar is sweet vinegar)
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the tuna well, then flake it into a bowl.
2. Add the remaining ingredients and mix well.
3. Use 1/4 cup to spread on one slice of bread or an open-faced sandwich, or between two slices of bread for a regular sandwich, or mound on crisp lettuce.
Note: I get a large bottle at the Far-east food market because it has a sweet flavor.
For a low-sodium version, omit salt and use unsalted canned tuna.
Food Exchange per serving: 1 1/2 Lean Meat Exchanges; CAL: 98; CHO: 2g; PRO: 11g; FAT: 4g; SOD: 279mg, CHO: 26mg.
Source: The Art of Cooking for the Diabetic by Mary Abbott Hess, R.D., M.S., and Katharine Middleton.
Brought to you and yours via Nancy O'Brion and her Meal Master.
